reviews.review_description: The story is great, I love everything about it! The characters, the different endings, all of it is amazing! Really the only thing I dislike about it is how much it costed, other than that it’s great! 10/10!!!
If you like visual novels, dont miss this game. Its totally overpriced though, compared to other games.

reviews.review_title: Fun if you enjoy visual novels
reviews.review_description:
I agree with a lot of the other reviews here; NOT stop once the first credits roll. Trust me!

Overall it’s a fun visual novel. The characters are likeable, the mystery is compelling, and the true ending is satisfying.

I wish they had taken much more advantage of the unique medium of VR a little more, I can think of several moments that could have been made more impactful, but when it is used it works pretty well. 

Definitely not a masterpiece, but if you enjoy visual novels I’m sure you’ll have a good time with it like I did.

personally, i am a total sucker for a good story. i will totally lose enormous amounts of sleep to read or watch something for the first or fifth time. that being said, i did get multiple headaches trying to do this with a vr. if you do this as well, make sure to take breaks! after the first play through i thought it was fun to watch the dialogue change with the character development, and i haven't completely finished it or even gotten close, but i already love this game so much. totally recommend!

reviews.review_title: Very immersive experience for a VN
reviews.review_description: Having lived 8 years in Asia I understandably became a fan of Manga. Tokyo Chronos has a very compelling story and leaves you with the desire to “turn to the next page”. You can really bridge the boundary of reality and fantasy with this one.

This can't be called a game or novel. You spend the entire time just reading so you can make a few choices. Then you complete the "game", but to unlock everything you just keep going back over the same boring story. I fell asleep multiple times trying to do this.

reviews.review_title: I'm a sucker for a redemption story
reviews.review_description:
I didn't know what to expect going in, but it turned out to be a VR Visual Novel about friendship and how our bonds link us, as well as what happens when you fail to communicate and be there for each other. You see through the eyes of the main protagonist as you try to figure out whodunit and what happened with your closest friends. The characters are vibrant and alive and pull you in right away. It leaves you sucked in till the very end.

My only complaint is a technical one, and that is during a flashback with Machikoji the translation gets VERY broken and almost unintelligible. But it's only one very small part (why I still gave it 5 stars) . The rest is fine.

After going through a roller coaster of emotions between happy, sad, upset, confused and love I finally got to see what a beautiful masterpiece this game was. I recommend it to everyone to try, the negative reviews are people that can't see past graphics or are lazy to get to the true ending or want interaction, although I respect their opinions. VR is still developing and growing so it's not perfect but this game really made me feel like part of a world and a story where I felt connected with each character, so if you can see past all negativity and see where this game truly shines then you won't be dissapointed! I really loved the true last ending of the game and made my heart feel whole and accomplished after expecting the worse. Made me feel like a hero for getting to the true end and saving everyone including myself! I thank the developers for creating such a beautiful novel and I look forward to more of such beautiful master pieces like this one!
Even after my first playing it dosent stop to amaze me

reviews.review_title: Fantastic! Aim for the final true ending
reviews.review_description:
"Tokyo Chronos" is a game that reaches out you in so many levels, thanks to each character and how they and their life is presented. Each character has their weaknesses and strengths but through their experience in the Chronos World, they are able to learn and discover what is important to them.  Many can relate.

And unlike other visual novels which have a plethora of choices, for the majority of the game, it’s primarily reading.  But don't look at this as a negative for that.

It's until you reach towards the end of chapter four, in which you are presented with the first good and a bad ending.  The first play through took me about 3-4 hours.

Assuming you did the first good ending (which is not exactly good, but tragic imho), it will then give you a chance to replay the chapters but this time, you get the "skip" ability to bypass large dialogue sections.  What you will want to do is load replay pivotal chapters involving each character.

But through this chapter replay, now added to the storyline are storylines for each of the characters (which you can explore their past, their memories) and each will have their good and bad ending.  You will need to do this second round of playing through each good and bad ending.  This second play through is about another four hours unless you skip all dialogue but hopefully you don't because then you will miss out on the story for each of these characters.

If you were able to playthrough each character's good and bad ending, then you can load the final chapter & now get the true ending.

For the most part, the dialogue & music is fantastic.  I was impressed by seeing alternate opening and closing themes but the voice acting in this game is well-done!

FINAL JUDGMENT:
I have read reviews on this game and surprisingly, reading from people who though the first playthrough is the actual ending.  It's not!

I absolutely love this game.  Prepare to laugh, prepare to cry and prepare to be captivated by this game on VR!

After completing the entire story, I thought I'd write a review. For anyone expecting choices in this, do not. This is basically an emersive story that the first choice isn't until hours/chapters into it. 

I wish there was more interaction within this, but I loved the story and found myself in tears at some points. I was grateful on review suggestions of taking certain routes to get to the true ending. 


This story took a long time to finish, but like a good book, it was worth it.


I hope they consider creating another game, but to give more movement or choices would really be amazing.

Please note that this is a visual novel (VN) and mainly consists of clicking through dialogue and there is no gameplay. I didn’t realize this fact and was fairly disappointed and would’ve given this game one star if it weren’t for the ending. At first it’s pretty boring as you’re simply clicking through an anime in VR. What saves this game is the ending (the true ending) which is extremely beautiful and gives you a sense of accomplishment. See more below to help you with the game (may contain spoilers).
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
A couple of things to help you out:
1. Always choose the left option to get through all the subchapters for the true ending. Once you choose left, you will always have the option to go back to the ‘right’ choice. I made the mistake of choosing right in the first choice and had to go back later on.
2. You have to play this game twice. The first time sets the stage but leaves many questions unanswered. The second time will answer everything and you will get the true ending. There are a lot of repeats but you can quickly click the dialogue away or use the skip feature. But there is new information intertwined in each ‘Re:chapter’ (the second play-through).
3. Only after going through the first chapters (aka chapters), the second chapters (aka Re:chapters) and the left choices (aka subchapters) will you be able to unlock the true ending.

SUPER SPOILER BELOW:
.
.
.
.
.
.
Without going into too much detail and ruining the ending, I wanted to point out that the main story is about suicide which may make some uncomfortable.



Overall I feel it’s worth it to try as long as you know what you’re getting into since the idea is quite novel and the ending is fantastic. I might even think about getting the sequel, Altdeus: beyond chronos, which looks more interactive. This is quite a surprise since after playing this game for a few hours I really didn’t think it was worth it but I was convinced by the end.

reviews.review_title: The Most Beautiful And Sad Game
reviews.review_description: This is one the most beautiful game that i have every played it had me crying at the good ending beautiful and sad

reviews.review_title: Pretty good, but gets boring fast
reviews.review_description:
Overall: I love the story, the graphics are nice, the voice acting is good, but it drags on for so long, and there are minimal choices. It feels like you’re watching a super long movie, but you’re stuck there, and you can’t look at something else while waiting for something to actually happen. When stuff happens, it’s good. But that doesn’t happen as often as it should. 

Animation: There’s barely any animation, the characters will just fade into a new pose. The most you’ll get is blinking and talking animations. Some characters don’t blink when they’re not talking to you. Their eyes do follow you though, which is nice

Interactivity: None. You stand there, and you make choices every few hours of gameplay. You can’t touch any of them, you have hands but they serve no purpose, they don’t even have any poses that change based on what buttons you hold. The characters look like they’re in the same space as you, until you try to move towards them. I don’t know how to explain it well, but it’s as if your walking doesn’t bring them as close as it should. And when you get closer, you realize they’re too big. Like you’re not a person, but a super tiny person on a really big tower, and these other people are tower sized. And if you stray too far (like, a couple feet) it’ll go black and tell you to return to where you were

Basically this game isn’t meant for VR. It’s a glorified PC game that you can look around in. The characters don’t feel like they’re real because they don’t move right and are missized. The story isn’t fast paced enough to warrant shutting out the rest of the world to experience. You barely ever get to do anything aside from hit the next button. 


Pretty disappointed as someone who desperately wants to experience what it would be like to be in an anime, but overall not awful. Just keep your expectations low.

This is a vn. That being said it's amazing and I'm not really a big reader. After you beat it the first time play it again to unlock more choices 

Always pic the left choice first don't worry you will immediately after have the ability to check the one on the right 


Pls don't pass this amazing game up

reviews.review_title: Not a game, not a great anime either
reviews.review_description:
Just to be clear as it’s not obvious from the description, this isn’t a game of any kind. It’s a novel, set in non interactive low fi 3D backgrounds with non animated characters who teleport in and out. I love anime, love manga, could not spend more than 10 minutes in this without being deeply disappointed. I can’t see why I’d put up with the discomfort of a VR helmet for this when there’s dozens of other better ways to consume the same kind of content!

The content itself is really bland too, deeply tropey stuff that you’ve seen a thousand times before. Highschool kids trapped in a limbo version of Shibuya? It’s clear the writer had no idea this would be a visual novel either, there are hundreds of lines of text explaining what you feel and see, but very little of that is actually in the graphics you’re actually seeing, so it’s laughable to pretend you’re feeling it.

Sigh. Wanted to like this, really didn’t, regret spending the money. Hope you don’t need to.

reviews.review_title: It is what it is, and I like it.
reviews.review_description:
This is an immersive visual novel so don't expect an Action game or RPG, or any agency. If you're fine with that and like a good story, you may like this game.

It's the immersion that sets this apart from visual novels in more typical formats. It's cool to be in a scene, and being able to look up and around 360-degrees between the dialogue. The story is engaging, and not completely predictable.

I think that on balance, it's worth the premium £30 price tag. Like an anime, there are a couple of obvious mistakes in the English subs, but the game is fully voice-acted (in Japanese obviously). I plan to buy the second game in the series, especially if goes on sale.

reviews.review_title: Best game ever. Changed me view on life.
reviews.review_description: This game is amazing idk why west people had to complain, im in the west btw, and I thought It was beautiful. The story was amazing I couldn't even go to bed because I just wanted to keep going and going its such an amazing game I wish this would get more recognition and if there would be merch for it I would like a shirt or something

The game itself, if you like visual novels, isn't bad. The story is interesting enough, there are different endings and finding them all is quite the journey. Decently long, too, after you've found them all there's no replayability at all but that's quite normal for a visual novel. The graphics could be decidedly better, but whatever. There is little to no animation, the game doesn't benefit much from being VR, the enjoyment - or lack thereof - would have been just the same in 2D.
What really pisses me off, though, is the translation of the subtitles, which is mostly perfect but is completely off in one of the sub chapters. Like, I didn't understand what was going on at all, there were weird symbols replacing some of the words, I'm pretty sure google translator would have done a better job. You can't sell a game saying it has english language available and then give me that translation, even though it's just one chapter. And it's fairly ahead with the story too, so you can't even return the game and get a refund becuase you've already played it too much. It's pretty dishonest if you ask me, and straight up unacceptable for the price. Now I'll never know what the heck happened in that chapter unless I learn to speak Japanese. 

Also, the price is a bit high anyway for a game that has like 20 hours top of gameplay and no replayability after them. I bought it when it was 50% off and I thought that was the right price before finding the translation problem.

Also, but that's not important, a little attention to details... MINOR SPOILER AHEAD: if someone get stabbed in the right arm, you can't have that certain someone hold their left arm in pain...

This should never have been made into a VR app. It’s a comic book that you have to put a head set on to read. There is no interactivity, nothing to do but click the button for the next page. According to other reviews at some point there is an opportunity to  make a choice but ONE choice. This could as easily have been created as a paper book with an instruction on which page to turn to.

reviews.review_title: I don't want to be a boy
reviews.review_description: Great game, great storyline The graphics are cool especially if you're into anime but the only downer for me personally is the character is a boy I wish I can change the sex of the character I didn't know I was going to be inside the body of a guy That's the only thing I wish I could change I just don't want to be a boy I finally find the kind of game I'm looking for but I'm a boy character and it's driving me crazy I understand the concept that this is his story that I'm inside of his body I thought it would be me in the game I just wish that was more clear before I bought the game if y'all do make another one can the main character please be a girl and we can have a choice on how we want them to look (sex ,race, hair clothing choice will make it fun but it can also be optional) thanks.
The language description is incorrect.
It is a great game with a fantastic story line. I played this game for two weeks played around one hour per day￼. Need more games like this!￼￼

reviews.review_title: no VR movement, no choice, bad graphic
reviews.review_description:
1. Multiple-choice MY ASSSS, this is a text reading and picture watching non-game, like a ppt presentation, and when I finally get to make a decision, the only option I have is to kill the person, WHY ask me to make a choice when there is only ONE option?????  

2. The graphic of the environment makes me feel like being inside a game of 90s

I seriously doubt the authenticity of the number of 5 star rating
I purchased this because it says it has Chinese, but in the game I cannot see this option. Anyone know how to solve it, or did I buy the wrong version?

reviews.review_title: We look forward to further evolution.
reviews.review_description: This is a new entertainment experience.

Went on sale on Bfriday for 40% then 15 days later (1 day after u couldn’t refund) went for 50% then New Years sale they bundled it with ALTDEUS that was messed up what you did there Couldn’t you have done that in the first place? So disappointed 👎

reviews.review_title: THIS GAME IS A RIPOFF AND A LIE.
reviews.review_description:
I tried to get a return immediately but was denied so here I go


SHORT REVIEW:  Bad writing, bad gameplay, bad everything. Not worth the money . There's way cheaper games with way better quality  Actually there's free games that are better than this. Play a PC novel instead. Just look up "The best anime visual novels" close your eyes and pick. And whatever you pick I guarantee it's cheaper and better than this CRAP  

LONG REVIEW: This game cost 40, when all it is, is 3D texture's talking,standing and sitting. There's barely any interaction. The story is bland and uninsteresting and a lot of it could of been removed or written better. 
The character's are all tell and don't show. Green haired lady is so smart and never wrong, purple haired femboy is a leader like and instead of experiencing  these features it's told to you when a character is acting strange or themselves. Why?? Because the character's are not written well, nothing is! The game is trailered to make it seem like it's a mystery, interactive novel but it's not. AND WHY THE F--K DO I HAVE HANDS IF THEY'RE JUST FOR DECORATION 95% OF THE TIME!?!?
I would rate it two probably if the price was lower.

reviews.review_title: goes down the drain at the end.
reviews.review_description:
There are a lot of motionless, and even black, screens with just text in one of the story arc's at the end. There is little or no VR enhancement in those parts. Did they ran out of budget to do that part properly ? 

In the Description of the game it states "To kill, or not to kill ─ YOU decide. "
So why can't i ? The decision is forced ?

For the few locations they have in the game the textures really should be better that this.

I would like to see more polished versions of these kind of games though.
Enjoyed it all the way up to the very end, and the ending is some sick stuff, very disappointing, turned it off and refused to finish it off, and no way of getting a refund.
Great story, great characters, great translations, great game!

reviews.review_title: If all VR VN are like this, sign me up
reviews.review_description:
I've just started it, but by the way this was put together I can already see I will love it.

Edit 1: OMG it's really good! The story is great and the VR makes you feel like you're really one of the characters, in the middle of the plot. Also, I don't know why some people criticize the 3D character design, I love it!

Edit 2: I've finished it. Unlocking the true ending had me stumped for a bit, but it was obvious in retrospect. I confirm my ★★★★★ rating! This is a piece of art and literature, well worth the price. I'm now looking forward to whatever MyDearest has in store next!
This is a MUST buy for any weeb. This game is literally a Visual Novel but implemented into a VR version. The story has such a nice and heartwarming story.

reviews.review_title: Hope to see more from this company
reviews.review_description: This is the next level of immersion for visual novels. I hope more developers get inspired by this.

reviews.review_title: Hated when I thought it was over....
reviews.review_description: Non content spoiler..... If you get to the end and feel like.... that's it?  Answer the choice both ways AND watch all the way through the credits.  It unlocks the rest of the story.....and it's a LOT.
Everything is there for the perfect visual novel

reviews.review_title: It’s What I Never Knew I Wanted
reviews.review_description: This virtual novel ( VN ) is so amazingly gripping it’s like watching some of my favorite anime. I grew up watching anime, Dragon Ball (Z, GT), Inuyasha, Naruto, etc... this blew me away with character design, music, environment, and feeling like the character that I was enveloped in. I could find myself thinking the very words he would say next. If you are into anime with an interesting story and a strange scary world. Welcome. This story will make you look over your virtual shoulder, you feel watched. You’ll also feel connected to your cast of friendly, funny, charming, and very well written characters. They are amazingly rendered; their models are crazy to look at if you’ve always watched anime. To finally be in an anime in this way was enough to make me tear up. Thank you to the staff, developers, and sponsors; just everyone that helped put this together. To everyone else; please try the game it’s 40$ for an experience that will stick with you for a very long time.

Tokyo Chronos is a Visual Novel, for anyone not family with the genre, it is a ‘choose your adventure’ type story, where you mostly watch the actions with occasional choices that direct the story. I am going to break this review into 2 parts: ‘story’ and ‘VR experience..


Story: no matter how pretty a visual novel is, storytelling is central to its quality and ‘Tokyo Chronos’ absolutely shines. On the surface would describe it as a ‘sci-fi murder mystery’, but it is so much more than that, it is every genre possible, something only ‘Sheakspear’s Hamlet’ could achieve. It is powerful and moving, the story is original and it doesn’t feel forced.  The game provides tremendous character depth, it allows you to compare how one sees themself and how others see the same person. The contrast is beautifully done. The ending is very satisfying and not what you think it is.


VR experience: The game is absolutely beautiful. The characters are beautifully designed, and you genuinely feel close them. The environment is absolutely incredible. I think I will re-play the game just so I can look around myself in some of the settings.

I am so glad i got this, an absolute most for any visual novel fan, and anyone who enjoys good storytelling in a new immersive environment.
Have Chinese in information but not in game
